verdict: 2026-07-04 18:00:14 PDT — My design sketch for the ACs would be: keep history as a capped aggregate array in the existing encrypted config blob; build/append/format/merge in pure helpers; have End capture the same aggregate snapshot the UI displays; immediately persist or queue the append until persistence is ready; and let the server union-preserve history on the existing PUT. The branch mostly follows that shape and does not add needless dependencies or a new store/endpoint. I found two shape issues. I attempted `npm test`, but the read-only sandbox blocked Vitest from writing Vite temp config under `client/node_modules/.vite-temp` (`EPERM`).

findings.title: Hydration gate can drop an End-session append
findings.claim: `addMeetingSummary` updates local state but only calls `saveRates` when `hydratedRef.current` is true. If a presenter starts and ends a real session before the initial `loadRates` resolves, the immediate flush is skipped; if that load returns `null`, no later state change triggers the debounced save. That leaves an AC1 data-loss path for the feature's primary artifact.
findings.alternative: Treat history append as a durable queued action: if hydration is not complete, keep pending summaries and merge/flush them once load finishes; alternatively gate End/session controls until hydration or explicit session-only persistence state is known. Keep using `/api/rates` plus the server merge-preserve logic.
findings.win: Removes the remaining quick-close/early-load loss path without adding a new endpoint or store, and makes “End with duration > 0 persists” true in every client lifecycle state.

findings.title: Summary headcount has a second source of truth
findings.claim: The displayed aggregate already uses `totals.attendeeCount`, including simple mode's explicit attendee override. The summary snapshot separately stores `participants.length`, so a simple-mode session with a manual N can save a total and $/min computed from one headcount while the history row reports another.
findings.alternative: Build the summary from the same aggregate snapshot used by the UI: use `totals.attendeeCount` for `headcount`, or pass `totals` into `buildMeetingSummary` and let the helper extract `attendeeCount` and `costPerSecond`.
findings.win: Deletes a parallel source of truth and makes the persisted/copyable summary describe the same final numbers the presenter saw.
